raw_91_honest_c3: F9 fix produces ZERO change on nexus 96 measurement — PRE-F9 and POST-F9 outputs are byte-identical (modulo timestamp). This is BECAUSE there are no s-id-mismatched same-field-list duplicates in this corpus; F9 fix is a no-op here. Any positive delta would require a corpus where the bug manifests.

mechanism: with PASS 0 fix, dict has 2 entries (audit-fingerprint + cycle-fingerprint) collapsed across the 6 files despite typeA using s1=audit/s2=cycle and typeB using s1=cycle/s2=audit. without PASS 0 the same census would produce 4 entries (per-id-per-fingerprint).
note: this fixture is too small for A19 to be net-positive (dict overhead 213B > savings on 858B input). The point is to demonstrate the CENSUS fix mechanism works.
raw_91_honest_c3: F9 PASS 0 fix improves CENSUS accuracy. Encode-time substitution still uses raw per-file s<N> keys; full saving realization requires follow-on PASS 0.5 (encode-side id rewrite). Deferred — first prove census correctness.

mechanism: 22 dict entries with schema-id diversity (s1..s9 across iter_NNN.jsonl files) — PASS 0 successfully collapsed cross-file equivalents that prior census missed
raw_91_honest_c3: this is the PRIMARY empirical evidence the F9 fix is load-bearing. n6 corpus has natural schema-id mismatch (different iter sequences declare schemas in different orders). Without F9 fix the dict would have had ~30+ near-duplicate entries and likely net-negative saving.
